summary: This topic covers the process of assigning a salary template, also known as a contract, to an employee. It includes searching for employees, selecting templates, and setting contract dates.
steps: Search for the desired employee by ID or name.
Select the employee using the checkbox.
Click the 'Assign Template' button at the bottom.
Choose an option: 'Copy from Salary Template' (if a previous contract exists or you want to use a standard template) or 'Assign' (if no previous contract).
If copying, select an existing template (e.g., With PF, Without PF, Without ESI PF) or an employee's previous contract.
Review and modify amounts or add/remove earning/deduction heads as needed for the new contract.
Click 'Calculate' to view the computed salary amount.
Enter the 'End Date' for the contract. The 'Start Date' will automatically be set after any previous contract's end date.
Click 'Assign' to finalize the contract assignment.
business_rules: A new contract's start date must be after the end date of any previously assigned contract for the same employee.
To assign a contract for a past month (e.g., November if December is already assigned), the previous contract(s) covering that period must be removed first.
important_notes: The 'Assign Salary Template' option is used to define an employee's salary structure or pattern for a specific period.
Standard salary templates (e.g., With PF, Without PF, Without ESI PF) are available for selection.
You can modify contract details (amounts, heads) for upcoming periods, even when copying from an existing template.
questions: How do I assign a new salary contract to an employee?
Can I copy an existing salary template for a new contract?
What happens if I need to assign a contract for a date in the past?
How do I set the duration for an employee's salary contract?
related_topics: Viewing an Employee's Assigned Salary Template (Contract)
